text:
Epilepsy Centre

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 21 October 2013

Dr Patel
South Thames Neurology Service
Outpatient Department, St George’s Hospital, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT

Dear Dr Patel,

Anne Taylor, DOB: 09-03-1995, Hospital No: Q784529 NHS No. 6352987410
24 Greenfield Road, London, N1 5QT

Diagnoses:

Combined generalised and focal epilepsy (patient has both generalised and focal epilepsy).

I reviewed Anne Taylor today in the neurology clinic. She attended with her mother and support worker. They report that over the past three weeks she has experienced about seven to nine seizures in the last three weeks. These have included brief generalised episodes with loss of awareness and stiffening, as well as focal seizures arising from sleep with behavioural arrest and lip-smacking lasting 30–60 seconds, followed by several minutes of confusion. No clusters or status events have been reported.

She remains on stable background medication per her carer’s record and there have been no recent dose changes. Rescue therapy has not been required in this interval, and there have been no emergency department attendances. Triggers noted by the family include sleep disruption and intercurrent stress. They have been keeping a seizure diary which corroborates the pattern described above.

On examination today she was alert, with normal cardiorespiratory observations and no focal neurological deficits. There were no tongue bites or injuries reported since the last visit. We discussed safety and adherence strategies, including maintaining good sleep hygiene and continuing the seizure diary. No device therapy is in place, and there is no history of prior cranial surgery.

Plan:
- Continue current antiepileptic regimen unchanged pending further review of the diary trends.
- Provide written seizure first-aid advice to family and support worker.
- Arrange routine bloods for antiepileptic drug levels and general monitoring.
- Follow-up in three months, sooner if seizure frequency escalates or new features emerge.

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: over the past three weeks she has experienced about seven to nine seizures in the last three weeks
about seven to nine seizures in the last three weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "over the past three weeks she has experienced about seven to nine seizures in the last three weeks." This provides a numeric range and a time window. Normalize to the allowed format as a range per a 3-week period. No other conflicting frequencies are provided, and no seizure freedom period applies.
